input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to the modern `.runsettings` format. They mention that after upgrading MSTest packages, the settings are being ignored. This is a known issue — `.testsettings` files are deprecated and no longer supported in modern MSTest. Let me look at their files to understand what they have, and then I can use the relevant skill to help them migrate.

Let me first look at the project files to understand what they have.

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **no longer supported** in modern MSTest/VSTest. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <MSTest>
    <AssemblyResolution>
      <Directory useLoadContext="true" />
    </AssemblyResolution>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.Coverage.DynamicCoverageDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Here's what changed and why:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>` in `<RunConfiguration>` |
| `<AssemblyResolution>` | Moved under `<MSTest>` node |
| `<DataCollector>` in `<AgentRule>` | Moved to `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` with updated URI (`/2.0`) |

**To use it**, either:
- Run: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- Or add to your `.csproj`:
  ```xml
  <PropertyGroup>
    <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
  </PropertyGroup>
  ```

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` once you've verified the new settings work. For per-test timeouts (instead of session-wide), use `[Timeout(30000)]` on test methods or classes.
